violations.text: No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Allergen advisory statement is lacking on menus and/or order point. Allergen advisory is required to inform consumers of their obligation to inform staff of any food allergies. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

Staff's food was observed stored on a cutting board with a knife. Employees shall not drink or eat from open food containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Dishes, utensils, sponge, and dish washing soap were observed in and around the hand-washing sink in the barista area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026
Staff was observed washing strawberries in handsink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Cases of eggs were observed soiled with condensate and debris leaking from walk-in cooler evaporator/condenser. Eggs were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). Sanitizer pouch was replaced during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Various foods in the walk-in cooler were observed held at 45-52 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Time/temperture abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Cold foams and cold pressed juices were observed held at 47-50 degrees Fahrenheit in Barista Lowboy. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Time/temperature abused foods were voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026
Tomato jam was observed held past 7 days. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Eggs are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below. Establishment is limited to cook/serve processes only until establishment has adequate refrigeration. Modifications to menu required. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

Dog was observed in food establishment during time of inspection. live animals may not be allowed in the premises of a food establishment. State guidance on service animals provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026
Dry food goods and single service items were observed stored on the floor in the basement. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses (rice scoop and barista utensils). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

-Walk-in cooler was observed at ranging ambient temperature of 47-51 degrees Fahrenheit. -2 door lowboy in barista area was observed at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Ice accumulation observed on internal right side of unit.No foods that require refrigeration for food safety to be stored in these units until properly adjusted and can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2026

The condensate from the walk-in cooler refrigeration unit is draining into multiple containers and onto food. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain or other approved methods. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
The door closer in the restroom was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
Duct leading to basement was observed with an accumulation of dust. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026

The floors in the walk-in cooler and in basement were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and/or stagnant water. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
Floors in kitchen were observed in poor repair. Physical facilities to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2025

Vacuum packaged raw salmon was observed in cold drawer with packaging still intact. Reduced oxygen packaged fish must be removed from the packaging prior to thawing. Fish was still frozen; per staff it was just removed from the freezer. Packages were placed back into freezer during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area and at exterior balcony. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2025
Floors in kitchen were observed in poor repair. Physical facilities to be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2025
Persons unnecessary to the food establishment were observed in the food preparation and food storage areas. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2025

Establishment does not have an individual with Rhode Island Certified Food Safety Manager license. Establishment has individual with food safety certificate. Establishment to register individual. Application provided during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/12/2025

Various foods were observed held between 50-57 degrees Fahrenheit in sandwich prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per staff only the corn, eggs, and fresh mozzarella cheese were left in the prep unit overnight. All other foods were fresh placed this morning. Foods that were out of temperature for an unknown amount of time were voluntarily disposed of. Foods that were placed in sandwich unit today were placed with ice and/or placed into other refrigeration. Sandwich Prep unit cannot be use to hold and store TCS foods until properly adjusted and can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2025

Prep unit was observed at an ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Prep unit was taken out of service to hold TCS/PHF foods. Unit must be adjusted/ repaired prior to holding TCS/PHF foods. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025
The condensate from the walk-in refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain in a proper manner. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025

Establishment does not have an individual with Rhode Island Certified Food Safety Manager license. Establishment has individual with food safety certificate. Establishment to register individual. Application provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/13/2025

Raw shelled eggs were observed stored above bacon in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Various foods in prep unit was observed held between 47-49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were assessed during time of inspection; foods that were out of temperature for an unknown amount of time were voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
Housemade pickled red onions were observed held past 7 days. Food was voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Prep unit was observed at an ambient temperature of 49 degrees Fahrenheit. Prep unit was taken out of service to hold TCS/PHF foods. Unit must be adjusted/ repaired prior to holding TCS/PHF foods. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris: 1. blender 2. shelving throughout kitchen 3. ice machine vent/filter 4. vent in walk-in cooler New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025
The condensate from the walk-in refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain in a proper manner. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025

The walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of dust and soil residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025
